visuals.title: OS Storage Management Overview
visuals.description: Visual overview of the storage hierarchy (registers to tape), file-system management, mass-storage management with disk scheduling, and the I/O subsystem including buffering, caching, and spooling

diagrams.note: Speed decreases and capacity increases from top to bottom; volatile above main memory, non-volatile below
OS provides a uniform logical view of storage through the file system
OS manages secondary storage allocation, free space, and redundancy
Hides hardware peculiarities; buffering holds data in transit, caching keeps fast copies, spooling manages slow device output
The three key areas the OS handles for storage
